steps.title: Spot the categorical claim
steps.content: The notes include one statement that generalizes about circumnavigations as a category: “Most circumnavigations include stops on land.” This is distinct from the specific examples and the definition—it's a claim about the class overall.

steps.title: Match the generalization to the answer choices
steps.content: D is the only choice that makes a categorical claim about circumnavigations—contrasting the typical case with an exception—thereby generalizing about the class as a whole. A, B, and C stick to specific details (boats, the definition, or individual sailors) without characterizing circumnavigations broadly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
